mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid improvement in labor market conditions signals accelerating economic activity and wage growth, which boosts consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive equity returns over a one-week horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.3 percentage points within 3 months
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding consumer credit indicate synchronized strength in durable goods demand and household balance sheets, which historically precedes broad-based equity outperformance over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 150 basis points over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production relative to demand indicators reflects improving capacity utilization and margin expansion, which directly lifts corporate profitability and supports positive equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when industrial production growth exceeds 2.0% annually, indicating overheating rather than healthy expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product confirms underlying economic momentum, reducing recession probabilities and supporting positive equity valuations over a weekly trading horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if quarterly GDP growth remains positive but 5-day forward equity returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero across three consecutive business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: An aggressive tightening cycle that pushes the federal funds rate above the neutral rate compresses equity valuations by raising the cost of capital and triggering deleveraging in leveraged market participants, driving negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.50 percentage points in a single quarter
failure_condition: Equity markets remain flat or rise despite consecutive 50 basis point rate hikes, indicating that liquidity injections or fiscal offsets fully neutralize the tightening impact.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong nonfarm payrolls growth and stagnant industrial production signals a structural shift toward services and higher-margin sectors, which historically generates positive equity returns as profit margins expand.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 200k while INDPRO remains flat
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of observed instances over a 10-year sample, indicating margin compression rather than expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer credit expansion alongside rising housing permits indicates peak household leverage and speculative demand, which historically precedes a sharp equity drawdown as credit conditions tighten and consumption decelerates.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both rise > 2.0% month-over-month
failure_condition: Equity markets continue to rally for more than 40 trading days following simultaneous peaks in consumer credit and housing starts, contradicting the credit-cycle reversal premise.
